screens.name: landing
screens.expectations: A polished marketing landing screen for a paid lawn-irrigation planning app. Must show: a clear app name/brand, a hero value proposition (water/money savings), a single obvious primary call-to-action button to start (e.g. 'Load My Property'), and no visible debug/test/placeholder UI. Should look premium and trustworthy enough that a homeowner would pay for the Pro export, not like an unfinished prototype.

screens.name: planner-empty
screens.expectations: The lawn planning screen right after entering the planner, before anything is drawn. Must show a map/grid area to draw on, a toolbar for switching between zone and head tools, and clear instruction text telling the user to draw their lawn. Should feel like a real design tool, not a bare wireframe.

screens.name: zone-drawn
screens.expectations: A drawn lawn zone shown as a shaded/colored polygon on the map, with the toolbar now offering head placement. Should look clean and precise, not glitchy or overlapping.

screens.name: auto-placed-plan
screens.expectations: The full results screen after auto-placing sprinkler heads: sprinkler head markers inside the drawn zone, a prominent estimated annual dollar savings figure, a property breakdown, and a monetization/upsell card (Pro Plan / shop list). This is the core paid-value screen — it must look premium, trustworthy, and worth paying for, with clean typography and spacing, not cluttered or cheap-looking.

screens.name: edit-head-panel
screens.expectations: A single sprinkler head has been placed and selected, opening an 'Edit Head' panel with controls (radius slider, arc/type selectors, a remove action). The panel should look like a clean, purpose-built editing tool with clear labels, not a raw unstyled form.

screens.name: checkout-cancel-toast
screens.expectations: A toast/banner is shown telling the user checkout was cancelled. It should look like a deliberate, calm, non-alarming notice — not a jarring red error or browser-alert style message, since cancelling isn't the user's fault and shouldn't feel like a failure.
